system.name: EduRights SL
system.purpose: EduRights SL helps teachers upload educational resources, allows administrators to review legal and ethical compliance, and enables students to access approved educational materials.
system.answer: EduRights SL is a legal-tech educational resource sharing platform designed to support lawful, ethical and accessible educational resource sharing. It includes teacher uploads, admin review, copyright checks, licensing information, JSON APIs, Digital Public Goods alignment and SDG-focused education access.

software_license.name: MIT License
software_license.answer: EduRights SL uses the MIT License for the software implementation. The MIT License was selected because it is simple, widely recognised, open-source friendly and supports reuse, modification and distribution while requiring preservation of the license notice.

why_mit_license.answer: The MIT License was selected for EduRights SL because it supports openness, educational innovation, reuse, modification and collaboration. It is suitable for an educational legal-tech platform because it allows other developers or institutions to adapt the system while still respecting the original license notice.

resource_license.answer: EduRights SL separates the software license from the license of uploaded educational resources. The software uses the MIT License, but each uploaded resource must have its own license information such as Creative Commons, MIT, Apache 2.0, GPL, institutional permission or no license. Resources with unclear licensing should be reviewed carefully before approval.

copyright.answer: EduRights SL supports copyright compliance by requiring copyright owner information, permission status and license type when educational resources are uploaded. Resources are not immediately published. They must pass admin review before students can access them.

ipr.answer: EduRights SL respects Intellectual Property Rights by recording ownership information, attribution details, permission status and license information for educational resources. The system is designed to protect creators while allowing lawful educational sharing.

it_law.answer: EduRights SL applies IT Law principles by managing digital content responsibly, requiring user authentication, supporting admin review, recording audit logs, protecting intellectual property rights, using open standards such as JSON and exposing data through APIs for interoperability.

electronic_records.answer: EduRights SL treats uploaded resources, user accounts, approval decisions, audit logs and API data as electronic records. These records help support transparency, accountability and legal traceability within the system.

platform_liability.answer: EduRights SL reduces platform liability by preventing direct public publication of uploaded resources. Teachers submit resources, administrators review the legal and ethical information, and only approved resources become available to students.

teacher_responsibility.answer: Teachers using EduRights SL are responsible for uploading educational resources with accurate title, category, copyright owner, permission status, license type and SDG information. They should not upload resources they do not have permission to share.

admin_review.answer: EduRights SL uses an admin review process to check uploaded resources before publication. Administrators can approve or reject resources based on copyright information, license type, permission status, ethical suitability and educational relevance.

student_access.answer: Students can access and download only approved resources in EduRights SL. This helps ensure that students receive educational materials that have passed legal, ethical and administrative review.

ethics.answer: EduRights SL promotes ethical resource sharing by requiring transparency, attribution, permission verification, admin review and responsible access to educational materials. The system discourages unauthorized copying and irresponsible distribution.

privacy.answer: EduRights SL should protect user data by collecting only necessary information, using authentication, limiting access by role and keeping sensitive user information secure. Future development can strengthen this through formal privacy policies and stronger data protection controls.

data_accessibility.answer: EduRights SL supports data accessibility by storing structured legal and resource information in reusable formats. The legal knowledge base is stored in JSON so it can be read by ChatBolt, APIs, future mobile apps, school portals or other systems.

json.answer: EduRights SL uses JSON as an open standard format for its legal knowledge base and API responses. JSON is human-readable, machine-readable, lightweight and widely supported, making it suitable for data accessibility and interoperability.

xml.answer: EduRights SL currently uses JSON as the primary open data format. XML can be added later as a future export format if integration with systems that require XML becomes necessary.

api.answer: EduRights SL supports APIs so system data can be accessed and reused by other systems. The legal knowledge base can be exposed through an endpoint such as /api/legal-knowledge, while approved resources can be exposed through resource API endpoints.

interoperability.answer: EduRights SL supports interoperability by using JSON, structured metadata and API endpoints. This allows future integration with school portals, mobile applications, university systems, digital libraries and government education platforms.

digital_public_goods.answer: EduRights SL aligns with Digital Public Goods principles by promoting educational access, open standards, interoperability, transparency and responsible reuse of digital resources. Its use of JSON and open APIs supports data reuse and integration.

sdg.answer: EduRights SL supports SDG 4, Quality Education, by improving access to educational resources while maintaining legal and ethical compliance. It also supports SDG 9 through digital infrastructure and SDG 16 through transparent governance and accountability.

audit_logs.answer: EduRights SL uses audit logs to record important actions such as login, upload, approval, rejection, deletion and downloads. Audit logs improve accountability, transparency and legal traceability.

governance.answer: EduRights SL governance is based on controlled uploads, admin review, copyright checks, license transparency, role-based access, audit logging, JSON-based knowledge management and API-driven interoperability.

open_standards.answer: EduRights SL uses open standards by storing knowledge and resource information in structured JSON format. This makes the data easier to access, reuse, integrate and extend in future systems.

future_expansion.answer: Future improvements for EduRights SL may include XML export, advanced document scanning, stronger privacy controls, integration with learning management systems, mobile app support, external API documentation and deeper legal compliance analytics.

chatbolt_scope.answer: EduRights ChatBolt only answers questions about EduRights SL legal compliance, licensing, copyright, Intellectual Property Rights, IT Law, Digital Public Goods, SDG alignment, JSON, APIs, interoperability, ethics and system governance.

out_of_scope.answer: EduRights ChatBolt is limited to EduRights SL legal, ethical, licensing, IPR, IT Law, DPG, SDG, JSON, API and compliance topics. It does not answer unrelated general questions.
